title: Mobile-First Dashboard Redesign
description:
Redesign Dashboard page using mobile-first approach with optimized layout and touch interactions.

Current Issues:
- Desktop-first design doesn't adapt well to mobile
- Small tap targets on mobile
- Cards too wide/narrow on different screens
- Statistics not easily scannable on mobile

Mobile-First Design (320px - 767px):
Subtasks:
в–Ў Stack stat cards vertically (1 column)
в–Ў Increase font sizes for readability (min 16px body)
в–Ў Increase tap targets to min 44px Г— 44px
в–Ў Bottom-aligned navigation/actions
в–Ў Sticky header with user info and logout
в–Ў Swipeable stat cards (carousel alternative)
в–Ў Recent tickets as cards (not table)
в–Ў Touch-friendly spacing (min 16px between elements)
в–Ў Optimize for one-handed use (important actions at bottom)

Tablet Design (768px - 1023px):
в–Ў 2-column stat card grid
в–Ў Recent tickets as cards (2 columns)
в–Ў Larger touch targets maintained
в–Ў Side-by-side quick actions

Desktop Design (1024px+):
в–Ў 4-column stat card grid
в–Ў Recent tickets as table
в–Ў Hover states and mouse interactions
в–Ў More information density

Responsive Components:
- StatCard: Adapts padding and font sizes
- ModuleCard: Touch-friendly on mobile, hover on desktop  
- Recent tickets: Cards on mobile, table on desktop
- User header: Collapsed on mobile, full on desktop

Tailwind Strategy:
- Base styles: Mobile (320px)
- sm: (640px) Small tablets
- md: (768px) Tablets
- lg: (1024px) Desktop

Files to Modify:
- frontend-tsklets/client/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx
- frontend-tsklets/client/src/components/StatCard.tsx
- frontend-tsklets/client/src/components/ModuleCard.tsx

CSS Variables for Touch Targets:
- --touch-target-min: 44px
- --spacing-mobile: 16px
- --spacing-desktop: 24px

Acceptance Criteria:
- Dashboard looks great on 320px (iPhone SE)
- Tap targets minimum 44px on mobile
- Smooth transitions between breakpoints
- Stats readable and scannable on all devices
- Recent tickets optimized for each screen size
- Performance: Fast render on mobile devices
- No horizontal scroll on any screen size

Estimated Time: 4 hours

title: Password Management & Security
description:
Force password changes on first login and implement strong password requirements.

Features:
- Password complexity validation (8 char, 1 upper, 1 lower, 1 number, 1 special)
- Change password modal on first login (blocks dashboard access)
- Password strength indicator (weak/medium/strong)
- Current password verification
- New password confirmation matching

Flow:
1. User logs in successfully
2. Check requirePasswordChange flag
3. If true, show change password modal (cannot dismiss)
4. User enters current password + new password + confirm
5. Validate password complexity
6. Update password, clear flag
7. Redirect to dashboard

Default Password: Systech@123
Triggers: First login OR admin password reset

title: Unified Work Items Schema
description:
Migrate from separate epics/features/devTasks tables to unified work_items polymorphic table.

## Schema Changes
- [ ] Create work_items table (polymorphic: epic/feature/task/bug/spike/requirement)
- [ ] Create work_item_links junction table (many-to-many with module/component/addon)
- [ ] Create work_item_assignments table (multiple assignees per item)
- [ ] Add product_sequences support for new issue types
- [ ] Create database migration scripts

## Data Migration
- [ ] Migrate existing epics вҶ’ work_items (type='epic')
- [ ] Migrate existing features вҶ’ work_items (type='feature')
- [ ] Migrate existing devTasks вҶ’ work_items (type='task'/'bug')
- [ ] Preserve all existing relationships and issue keys
- [ ] Verify data integrity post-migration

## API Updates
- [ ] Create unified /api/work-items CRUD endpoints
- [ ] Update /api/products/:id/dashboard to use new schema
- [ ] Maintain backward compatibility with existing endpoints (deprecate gradually)
- [ ] Update TypeScript types in @tsklets/types

## Rollback Plan
- [ ] Keep old tables during transition period
- [ ] Feature flag for new vs old schema queries

title: Enterprise Product Platform Architecture
description:
Transform Tsklets from single-product support tool into enterprise product portfolio management platform.

## Vision
- Support multiple products (Tasklets, HRMS, CRM, MMS, Textile, etc.)
- Unified work tracking (EpicвҶ’FeatureвҶ’Task) with Beads integration
- Product structure hierarchy (ProductвҶ’ModuleвҶ’ComponentвҶ’Addon)
- Enterprise-grade analytics and reporting

## Architectural Decisions
1. **Modular Monolith** - Single codebase, shared DB, clear boundaries
2. **Unified Work Items** - Polymorphic table for Epic/Feature/Task/Bug
3. **Many-to-Many Linking** - Work items can span Module/Component/Addon
4. **Beads Import** - One-way import, extend with app-specific fields
5. **3-Tier Analytics** - Real-time + Daily snapshots + Monthly archives

## Scope
- Schema redesign for unified work items
- Beads import system
- Analytics engine with historical metrics
- Enhanced product dashboard

## Out of Scope (Future)
- Multi-tenant dashboards
- Cross-product work sharing
- Federated architecture

## Statuses (7 total)

| Status | Owner | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| pending_internal_review | Client | New ticket, internal triage queue |
| open | Systech | Pushed to Systech, SLA active |
| in_progress | Systech | Systech actively working |
| waiting_for_customer | Systech | Systech needs info from client |
| rebuttal | Systech | Systech pushes back, needs discussion |
| resolved | Systech | Fixed, waiting for client to close |
| closed | Client | Ticket complete |

---

## Labels

| Label | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| escalated | Urgent, needs immediate attention (different from normal push) |
| resolved_internally | Closed without Systech involvement |
| auto_closed_no_response | Cron job closed after 5 days of resolved/waiting_for_customer |
| internal_assignment | Assigned to employee for clarification |
| created_by_systech | Systech created ticket on behalf of client |
| reassigned_to_internal | Systech sent back to client for internal handling |

---

## Push vs Escalate

| Action | Meaning | Result |
|--------|---------|--------|
| Push to Systech | Normal handoff, company_admin needs Systech help | Status вҶ’ open, SLA starts |
| Escalate | Urgent, needs immediate attention | Status вҶ’ open + label: escalated, SLA starts, visual urgency |

---

## Escalation Reasons (required when escalating)

| Reason | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| executive_request | Executive or management involvement |
| production_down | Production system is down/critical |
| compliance | Regulatory or compliance deadline |
| customer_impact | Multiple customers affected |
| other | Free text explanation required |

---

## Reassign to Internal (Systech action)

When Systech determines a ticket should be handled internally by the client:

1. Systech clicks "Reassign to Internal"
2. Adds a comment explaining why
3. Status вҶ’ `pending_internal_review`
4. Label `reassigned_to_internal` added
5. SLA paused (no longer Systech's responsibility)
6. Ticket appears back in company_admin's internal queue

---

## Rebuttal Flow

When Systech disagrees or needs clarification before proceeding:

1. Systech marks ticket as `rebuttal`
2. Client reviews and either:
   - Adds clarification вҶ’ Systech moves to `in_progress`
   - Agrees with rebuttal вҶ’ Client closes ticket

---

## Permissions Matrix

| Action | Employee | Company_Admin | Systech |
|--------|----------|---------------|---------|
| Create ticket | вң… | вң… | вң… (on behalf) |
| View company tickets | вң… (all) | вң… (all) | вң… (all companies) |
| Add comment | вң… | вң… | вң… |
| Assign internally | вқҢ | вң… | вқҢ |
| Push to Systech | вқҢ | вң… | вқҢ |
| Escalate (with reason) | вқҢ | вң… | вқҢ |
| Resolve internally | вқҢ | вң… | вқҢ |
| Change status (in_progress, etc.) | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… |
| Reassign to internal | вқҢ | вқҢ | вң… |
| Close ticket | Own only | вң… (any company) | вқҢ |
| Add watcher (user) | Self only | вң… (company users) | Self only |
| Add watcher (email) | вқҢ | вң… | вқҢ |

---

## Watchers

- **Purpose**: Track users/emails to notify when email/SMS is implemented
- **Storage**: ticket_watchers table (user_id nullable, email for non-users)
- **Scope**: Same company users + external emails (company_admin only)
- **Notifications**: Deferred to future implementation

---

## SLA Rules

| Event | SLA Timer |
|-------|-----------|
| Ticket created | вқҢ Not started |
| Pushed to Systech | вң… SLA STARTS |
| Escalated | вң… SLA STARTS (with urgency flag) |
| Reassigned to internal | вҸёпёҸ SLA PAUSED |

---

## Auto-Close Cron

- **Trigger**: 5 days after ticket is in `resolved` or `waiting_for_customer`
- **Action**: Close ticket with label `auto_closed_no_response`
- **Frequency**: Daily cron job

---

## Key Decisions Made

1. All employees can see all company tickets (transparency)
2. Systech can see all tickets anytime (no restrictions)
3. Only client (reporter or company_admin) can close tickets
4. Push is one-way (cannot take back from Systech)
5. Escalation is a label, not a different SLA (someone follows up)
6. Escalation requires a reason (dropdown + optional text)
7. Systech can reassign back to internal if ticket shouldn't be with them
8. Watchers tracked now, notifications implemented later
